synopsis:
As the war against the Yuuzhan Vong grinds on, the living planet *Zonama Sekot* becomes the key to victory. On the conquered capital of Yuuzhan'tar, Nom Anor, still masquerading as the heretic prophet Yu'shaa, is losing the faith of his underground cult of Shamed Ones. Recalling that Supreme Overlord Shimrra dreads the rumored living world, Anor proclaims that on the day of Shimrra's fall a great planet will appear in the skies to herald a new age. Master Shaper Nen Yim, meanwhile, has been ordered to study a captured ship from Zonama Sekot and discovers it combines biological and mechanical components. Anor strikes a bargain with her: he will summon Tahiri Veila to spirit them both off Yuuzhan'tar and take them to the living world to learn its nature. The priest Harrar, secretly opposed to Shimrra, joins them.

Tahiri, on Mon Calamari, accepts the call and brings Corran Horn. The group escapes aboard the Sekotan ship and reaches Zonama Sekot, where the vessel dies and the three Yuuzhan Vong renounce their people's violent ways. Tahiri and Nen Yim realize that the memories once implanted in Tahiri by Mezhan Kwaad in fact came from Nen Yim. As the shaper begins to grasp the planet's true nature, Nom Anor sees a chance to redeem himself in Shimrra's eyes: he murders Nen Yim and descends into the planet's depths to sabotage its hyperdrive and kill it. Dying, Nen Yim warns Tahiri of his intent. Tahiri, Corran, and Harrar pursue him; Harrar is knocked from a cliff and presumed dead, and Anor, having secretly summoned an escort of Yuuzhan Vong vessels, is carried back to Shimrra. But Sekot casts off the sabotage and heals itself, then jumps to hyperspace with a destination of its own choosing: Yuuzhan'tar. A subplot follows Operation Trinity, the campaign to retake the Bilbringi system, and Jaina Solo's capture by pirates while Wedge Antilles commands one of its fleets.
title: The Final Prophecy
